CSCE 310 Syllabus

Section 501 (58342)

Database Systems

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

Meeting Times:  Meeting Type: LEC

Meeting Days: TR

Start Time: 11:10AM

End Time: 12:25PM

Start Date: 01/12/2026

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  ZACH 310

Credit Hours:  3

Instructor Details

Muhammad Mahmood

Email:  suleman.mahmood@tamu.edu

Office Location:  PETR 109

Phone:  9798452741

Office Hours

See course calendar on canvas

Catalog Description

Database Systems. (3-0). Credit 3. File structures and access methods; database

modeling, design and user interface; components of database management

systems; information storage and retrieval, query languages, high-level language

interface with database systems. Prerequisites: CSCE 221 with a grade of C or

better; junior or senior classification.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  CSCE 221 with a grade of C or better; junior or senior

classification.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Explain the fundamental concepts of database systems, including data

models, database architecture, and the role of database management

systems (DBMS)

Design and implement Entity-Relationship (ER) and Enhanced Entity-

Relationship (EER) models to represent real-world data requirements

Apply normalization theory and functional dependencies to design efficient

and well-structured relational database schemas

Write complex SQL queries to create, manipulate, and retrieve data from

relational databases

Evaluate and compare different database paradigms, including relational

databases, NoSQL systems, and distributed databases

Analyze data warehouse architectures and understand their application in

business intelligence and decision support systems

Implement database connectivity in applications and apply security principles

to protect database systems

Special Course Designation

None

This material Is: Required

Component Specific Policies

Class Activities

Class activities will be graded based on participation rather than correctness.

They will only be available for completion during class time.

In case of excused absences, the activity will be marked excused. Canvas

processes excused absences by increasing the weight of the unexcused activities.

Three unexcused absences are allowed during the semester. This will be

processed at the end of the semester.

Homework

Homeworks are individual assignments and must be completed.

Collaboration with other students and the use of AI tools are strictly prohibited.

Regrade Requests

If you want to challenge any grading, you must do so within one week of grade release.

Regrade period for assignments published after week 13 will be shorter and will be

announced at the time of grade release. There may be no regrading period for the final

exam.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late submissions are only allowed for Homeworks.

There will be 10% per day penalty for late submissions.

There may be no late submission period for the final homework of the semester.

Final exam times are based on the  TAMU official final exam schedule . If there is an error

here, we will adhere to the TAMU official schedule.

AI Statement

Some homework problems and class activities will require use of AI to generate

code. The scope of AI use will be defined in the problems. In principle, you may

submit AI-generated code, or code that is based on or derived from AI-generated

code, as long as this use is properly documented in the comments: you need to

include the prompt and the significant parts of the response. AI tools may help you

avoid syntax errors, but there is no guarantee that the generated code is correct. It

is your responsibility to identify errors in program logic through comprehensive,

documented testing. Moreover, generated code, even if syntactically correct, may

have significant scope for improvement, in particular regarding separation of

concerns and avoiding repetitions. The submission itself must meet our standards

of attribution and validation.

Canvas

Dashboard for the entire course. This will link out to appropriate resources.

Campuswire

Campuswire will be the main forum to get help with homework and lab activities.

You will be added to the discussion forum at the start of the semester.

Whenever possible, post your questions publicly so that all students can benefit

from it. Please do not post your code and solutions in public posts. Public posts

can also be answered by other students so you should have a shorter response

time for them.

We will endorse good student responses.

You have the option to post anonymously. Your post will only be anonymous to

students. Instructors will be able to see your name and id on the posts.

Please be respectful.

Private messages can be posed to all instructors or an individual instructor.

Private messages to individual instructors are more likely to be overlooked and

should not be used. Send an email directly to the instructor.

Posts directed to instructors that would benefit the entire class and should be

visible to all students will be updated so that the post is visible by the entire class.

Google Drive

Google folder will be used to share lecture notes and in-class examples. You must be

logged into your TAMU Google account to access these materials or submit Google

Forms. Your TAMU Google account will have to be the default account in your browser,

or you can open an incognito window and log in from there. It might take up to 24 hours

after registering for the class to be able to access materials in the Google shared folder.

Zoom

Zoom will be used for online office hours, and meetings with instructors and teaching

assistants. Links will be provided as needed.
